subject: [PATCH] input/evdev: move kill_fasync() outside buffer_lock to fix SOFTIRQ deadlock

reviews.author: Dmitry Torokhov
reviews.summary:
reviewer noted that the patch does not actually fix the SOFTIRQ deadlock because it still acquires fa_lock while holding buffer_lock in evdev_pass_values(), and requested the lock be dropped before calling wake_up_interruptible_poll()

reviewer questioned whether the patch actually fixes the issue, pointing out that the code runs while holding input->event_lock with IRQs disabled
sentiment_signals: questioning the effectiveness of the fix
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Rik,

On Wed, May 13, 2026 at 11:50:00AM -0400, Rik van Riel wrote:

---

Does this really fix anything? This code is running holding
input->event_lock with IRQs off...

Thanks.

-- 
Dmitry

2026-05-13.patch_summary: This patch fixes a potential deadlock in the Linux kernel's input/evdev driver by moving the call to kill_fasync() outside of the buffer_lock spinlock. The buffer_lock is SOFTIRQ-safe, but kill_fasync() acquires a SOFTIRQ-unsafe lock, creating a potential ordering violation that can lead to a deadlock. By moving the kill_fasync() call after the buffer_lock is released, the patch resolves this issue and prevents the deadlock from occurring.

reviews.author: Rik Riel (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that the SOFTIRQ deadlock issue is caused by kill_fasync() acquiring a SOFTIRQ-unsafe lock, and agreed to restructure in v2.

The author acknowledges that the original patch does not fix the SOFTIRQ deadlock issue, and is seeking feedback on whether a suggested alternative (Sashiko) would work or if there's a better approach.
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed, 13 May 2026 13:01:58 -0700
Dmitry Torokhov <dmitry.torokhov@gmail.com> wrote:

---

You're right. The bug is real, but this patch does not fix it.
Would the Sashiko suggestion work, or is there a better way to
tackle it?

For reference, the lockdep splat is below:

=====================================================
WARNING: SOFTIRQ-safe -> SOFTIRQ-unsafe lock order detected
7.0.0-rc6-00259-g427a4f9708ee #82 Not tainted
-----------------------------------------------------
syz.6.15929/30382 [HC0[0]:SC0[0]:HE0:SE1] is trying to acquire:
ffff888101324558 (&new->fa_lock){...-}-{3:3}, at: kill_fasync_rcu fs/fcntl.c:1135 [inline]
ffff888101324558 (&new->fa_lock){...-}-{3:3}, at: kill_fasync fs/fcntl.c:1159 [inline]
ffff888101324558 (&new->fa_lock){...-}-{3:3}, at: kill_fasync+0x137/0x590 fs/fcntl.c:1152

and this task is already holding:
ffff88812f3d8028 (&client->buffer_lock){....}-{3:3}, at: spin_lock include/linux/spinlock.h:341 [inline]
ffff88812f3d8028 (&client->buffer_lock){....}-{3:3}, at: evdev_pass_values.part.0+0xf6/0x950 drivers/input/evdev.c:261
which would create a new lock dependency:
 (&client->buffer_lock){....}-{3:3} -> (&new->fa_lock){...-}-{3:3}

but this new dependency connects a SOFTIRQ-irq-safe lock:
 (&dev->event_lock){..-.}-{3:3}

... which became SOFTIRQ-irq-safe at:

Chain exists of:

2026-05-14.patch_summary: This patch fixes a potential deadlock in the Linux kernel's input/evdev driver by moving the call to kill_fasync() outside of the buffer_lock spinlock. The buffer_lock is SOFTIRQ-safe, but kill_fasync() acquires a SOFTIRQ-unsafe lock, creating a potential ordering violation that can lead to a deadlock. By moving the kill_fasync() call after the buffer_lock is released, the patch resolves this issue and prevents the deadlock from occurring.
